question: In a three-way joint venture, the first investor contributes capital, the second contributes time and strategy, and the third brings a network of suppliers. They agree to share profits in the ratio 3:2:2. If the venture earns Rs. 1,40,000 in the first year, how much should the strategist (second partner) receive?

The investments of three partners, P, Q, and R, are initially in the ratio 6 : 7 : 9. After a year, P adds Rs30,000, Q adds Rs25,000, and R adds some amount to their respective investments. The new ratio of their investments becomes 8 : 9 : 11. Find the amount (in Rs) added by partner R.
A, B, and C invested in a business in the ratio 2:3:5. After a year, the total profit is Rs. 1,00,000. What is B's share?

A startup has three stakeholders. A brought 40% of the capital, and B brought the rest. C joined later and was promised 20% of the total profit for branding. If total profit was Rs. 2,50,000, how much was B's share?
If the ratio of capital of A and B is 3:2 and the time for which they invested is in the ratio 2:3, then the profit ratio is:
A and B invested Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 40,000 respectively for 12 months. A take 10% of the profit for managing. The remaining profit of Rs. 90,000 is divided based on capital. What is B's share?

Partners X and Y entered into a business investing Rs. 50,000 and Rs. 1,00,000 respectively. After 6 months, Z joins with Rs. 1,50,000. At year-end, profit was Rs. 90,000. How much more did Z earn than X?

A and B started a business by investing Rs. 75,000 and Rs. 45,000 respectively. After 3 months, C joined with Rs. 60,000. At the end of the year, what is C's share in a profit of Rs. 40,000 (approx.)?
A, B, and C invest in the ratio 4:5:6. The time ratio is 3:2:1. What is the ratio of their profits?

D and E start a business with equal investments. After 4 months, D adds Rs. 10,000 more. At the end of 1 year, if the total profit is Rs. 33,000, and E's share is Rs. 15,000, what was their initial investment?

M and N start a business with Rs. 2,00,000 and Rs. 3,00,000 respectively. After 4 months, M adds Rs. 1,00,000 more, and N withdraws Rs. 50,000. At the end of 1 year, they make a profit of Rs. 1,80,000. What is N's share of profit?
Partners X and Y invest Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 90,000 respectively. X works for 6 months, Y for 4 months. Profit is divided based on capital * time. Find their profit ratio.

A' and 'B' entered a joint venture. 'A' brought Rs. 1,50,000 and 'B' Rs. 2,00,000. 'A' also contributed skilled labor equivalent to Rs. 50,000. At the end of the year, they earned Rs. 1,20,000 in profit. If they agreed to divide the profit in proportion to their effective contributions, what was A's share?

A', 'B', and 'C' formed a consulting firm. Their capital investments were Rs. 6,00,000, Rs. 4,00,000, and Rs. 5,00,000, respectively. After 3 months, 'A' withdrew Rs. 1,00,000, and 'B' added Rs. 1,00,000. The partnership lasted for 9 months. What was the ratio of their effective capital contributions?
A and B share profit in 7:5. If B gets Rs. 15,000, how much did A receive?
If two partners invest for the same period but share profits in 2:3, and the total profit is Rs. 15,000, what is the capital of the second partner if the first invested Rs. 10,000?

Three friends, X, Y, and Z, invested in a start-up in the ratio 2:3:5. X managed the business and was entitled to an additional 15% of the total annual profit as a management fee. If the profit at the end of the year was Rs. 4,00,000, how much did Y receive?

Three partners, A, B, and C started a firm with investments in the ratio 3:2:4. After 6 months, A doubled his capital, B halved his, and C maintained his investment. If the total profit after one year was Rs. 1,80,000, what was B's share of profit?
P starts a business with Rs. 8,000. Q joins after 5 months with Rs. 12,000. If the profit at the end of the year is Rs. 6,800, what is Q's share (approx.)?

A, B, and C invest in a business in the ratio 2:3:5. After 4 months, A increases his capital by 50%, B decreases his capital by , and C leaves the capital unchanged. At the end of the year, if the total profit is Rs. 33,000, find the share of B.

Three partners share the profit in a business in the ratio 6:8:9. They invested for 14 months, 8 months, and 7 months, respectively. What is the ratio of their investments? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]
If 3(P's Capital) = 4(Q's Capital) = 7(R's Capital), then out of the total profit of Rs. 3150, R will receive: [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]

A, B, and C enter into a partnership. A invests Rs. 80,000, B invests Rs. 60,000, and C invests Rs. 40,000. After 6 months, B increases his capital by 25%, while C decreases his capital by 10%. At the end of 1 year, the total profit is Rs. 1,20,000. What is C's share in the profit? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

A, B, and C started a business. A contributed Rs. 12,000, B contributed Rs. 15,000, and C contributed Rs. 18,000. After 6 months, B withdrew Rs. 5,000, while C added Rs. 10,000. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs. 54,000, how much did C receive?

A, B, and C enter into a partnership. A invests Rs. 80,000, B invests Rs. 60,000, and C invests Rs. 40,000. After 6 months, B increases his capital by 25%, while C decreases his capital by 10%. At the end of 1 year, the total profit is Rs. 1,20,000. What is C's share in the profit? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

P, Q, and R started a business. P is the working partner and receives a monthly salary of $2,000 from the total profit. The remaining profit is distributed among all three in the ratio of their investments. P, Q, and R invested $50,000, $60,000, and $70,000 respectively. If at the end of the year, P's total earning was $40,000, what was the total profit?

A, B, and C started a business. A invested Rs. 1,20,000, B invested Rs. 80,000, and C invested Rs. 1,00,000. After 6 months, A added Rs. 20,000, and C withdrew Rs. 10,000. If the total profit at the end of 2 years is Rs. 3,60,000, how much will A receive?
A and B invest Rs30,000 and Rs50,000 respectively. After 6 months, C joins with Rs60,000. What will be the ratio of their profits at the end of the year? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]
A, B, and C invest Rs20,000, Rs30,000, and Rs50,000 respectively in a business. The profit at the end of the year is Rs50,000. What is B's share of the profit? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]
A and B invest Rs50,000 and Rs40,000 respectively. After 6 months, A withdraws all of his capital. At the end of the year, what is their profit-sharing ratio? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-3]
A started a business with Rs30,000. B joined after 5 months with Rs40,000. At the end of the year, what is their profit-sharing ratio? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-3]
Two investors, A and B, invest in the Business in the ratio of 4:5. If the share of B is Rs 1000 in profit. Find the total profit.
A, B, and C invest Rs18000, Rs12,000, and Rs20,000 respectively for a year. What percentage of the total profit will C get?
A and B invest Rs20,000 and Rs30,000 in a business, B receives Rs1000 more than A from the annual profit. What is the total profit?
A and B started a business with investments of Rs5000 and Rs4000, respectively. After 6 months, A added Rs1000 and B added Rsx. If the profit ratio is 11:18, find x.
A started a business with Rs30,000. B joined after 5 months with Rs40,000. At the end of the year, the total profit was Rs80,000. What is the share of A?
A, B and C start a business. A invests Rs5000 for 12 months, B Rs4000 for 9 months, and C Rs3000 for 6 months. If the total profit is Rs3800000, find A's profit share.
A, B, and C are partners in a Business. A and B are sharing the ratio in 3:4, and B: C are sharing in 5:9. At the end of the year, the profit received is Rs 71000. What is the share of C?
In a partnership, the capital ratio between A and B is 5:7. If A invested Rs 50,000, how much did B invest?
A started a business with Rs30,000. B joined after 6 months with Rs40,000. At the end of the year, what is the profit-sharing ratio?
In a partnership, A and B invest in the ratio 4:5. If the total profit is Rs. 1,10,000, how much profit does A get?
In a partnership, P, Q and R invested Rs 50,000, Rs 60,000, and Rs 70,000 respectively. If the total profit is Rs 24,000, how much will Q receive?
A and B started a business with Rs12000 and Rs18000. B left after 4 months. Find their profit ratio after 1 year.
A and B invested Rs. 40,000 and Rs. 60,000, respectively. At the end of 6 months, the total profit is Rs. 40,000. What is B's share in the profit?
A started a business with Rs30,000. B joined after x months with Rs40,000. At the end of the year, their profit-sharing ratio is 3:2. What is the value of x?
The profits of three partners are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. If their profits increase by 50%, 40%, and 30%, what is the new ratio of profits?
A, B, and C start a business. A invests Rs5000 for 12 months, B Rs4000 for 9 months, and C Rs3000 for 6 months. If profit is Rs380000, find the share of B.
If P and Q invested in a business, and the profit-sharing ratio is 3:4, and P invested Rs 30,000, how much did Q invest?
A, B, and C invest Rs20,000, Rs30,000, and Rs50,000, respectively, in a business. The profit at the end of the year is Rs50,000. What is A's share of the profit?
A, B, and C invest in the ratio 2:3:5. If the total profit is Rs10,000, find B's share.
A, B, C invest in 2:3:5. If profit is Rs10000, find A's share.
A and B invested Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 90,000, respectively. If the profit at the end of the year is Rs. 1,50,000, what is B's share?
A and B invested Rs10,000 and Rs15,000 respectively. If the business made a profit of Rs12,000, what is A's share?
A, B and C have invested in a business with the ratio of investments as 5:3:2. If the total profit for the year is Rs. 150,000, what amount does B receive?

Three friends start a mobile app company. Arjun invests 1,20,000, Beena invests 90,000, and Charu invests 1,50,000. After one year, they earn a profit of 72,000. What will Beena's share of the profit be?
Riya invests 60,000 and Kabir invests 80,000. At the end of the year, they earn a profit of 70,000. How much profit does Kabir get?
A, B, and C are partners in a Business. A and B are sharing the ratio in 3:4 and B: C are sharing in 5:9. At the end of the year, the profit received is Rs 71000. What is the share of A?

A and B started a business with initial investments of Rs. 50,000 and Rs. 1,00,000, respectively. After 6 months, C joined with an investment of Rs. 1,50,000. If the total profit at the end of 1 year is Rs. 4,50,000, how much will C receive?
Aman and Binoy enter into a partnership with capitals in the ratio 4 : 5. If the total profit is Rs 9,000, what will Aman's share be?
Three partners P, Q, R invest in a business. Q's capital is three times R's capital and half of P's capital equals the capital of Q. If the total profit is Rs. 24000, find P's share in the profit.
A and B invested Rs. 10,000 and Rs. 20,000 respectively, in a business. If the total profit is Rs. 45,000, how much will A receive?
A and B start a business. A invests Rs2500 for 12 months, B invests Rs3000 for 10 months. Who gets more profit?
A and B invest in a business in the ratio 3:1. If 5% of the total profit goes to charity and A's share is Rs. 855, what is the total profit?
A and B invest in a business in the ratio 7:3. If the total profit earned after a year is Rs. 1,00,000, how much profit will B receive?

A, B, and C enter into a partnership. A invests Rs. 4,000 at the start. B invests Rs.6,000 after 4 months. C invests Rs.8,000 after 8 months. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs.1,650, what is the share of C?
A, B, and C invest in a business. A invests Rs 20,000, B invests Rs 25,000, and C invests Rs 30,000. What is the total profit if the share of B is Rs 5,000?
A and B invested Rs. 15,000 and Rs. 25,000, respectively. After 6 months, A withdrew Rs. 5,000. If the total profit after 1 year is Rs. 60,000, how much does A receive?
Chetan invested Rs. 9,000 for 12 months, and Sahir invested Rs. 6,000 for 6 months. If the total profit of the business is Rs. 18,000, how much will Chetan receive?
A and B invested Rs. 20,000 and Rs. 30,000, respectively. The profit at the end of the year is Rs. 60,000. What is B's share of the profit?
A, B, and C invested Rs. 50,000, Rs. 40,000, and Rs. 30,000, respectively. If the total profit after 1 year is Rs. 90,000, how much will B receive?

A, B, and C invested in a business in the ratio of 5:3:2. After 6 months, A doubled his investment, B increased his investment by 50%, and C kept his investment the same. If the total profit at the end of 1 year is Rs. 90,000, how much will B receive?

A, B, and C invest in a business with their capitals in the ratio 2:3:4. After 6 months, A increases his capital by 50%. At the end of 2 years, the total profit earned is Rs. 90,000. How much profit does A receive? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]

Three partners A, B and C start a business. A invests 80,000 for the entire year. B invests 60,000 but joins the business after 4 months. C invests 40,000 for the entire year. At the end of the year, the total profit is 1,08,000. Find the share of B in the profit.

X and Y started a business investing Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 90,000 respectively. After 5 months, X withdrew half his capital, and Y added Rs. 30,000 more. If the total profit at year-end is Rs. 49,500, find X’s share.

A, B, and C started a business investing Rs. 50,000, Rs. 70,000, and Rs. 90,000, respectively. After 6 months, C withdrew half of his capital. At the end of the year, what is the ratio of their profits?

P, Q, and R invested in a venture in the ratio 6 : 7 : 9. R, being a silent partner, is entitled to only half of what he would get as an active partner. If the total profit earned is Rs48,000 and 20% of it is reinvested, how much profit does Q receive?

Three partners Arun, Bablu, and Chandan invest Rs. 2,40,000, Rs. 1,60,000, and Rs. 4,00,000 respectively. Bablu is working partner and receives 30% extra profit as commission. Total profit is Rs. 75,000. What is Bablu's total share?
Mahesh started a business with Rs. 75,000. After 8 months, Suresh joined with Rs. 90,000. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs. 84000, what is Mahesh's share?
Amit, Bhupendra, and Chameli invest Rs. 30,000, Rs. 40,000, and Rs. 70,000 respectively. If total profit is Rs. 56,000, what is Bhupendra share?

X, Y, and Z invest in a partnership. Their capitals are in the ratio . X and Y invest their amounts for 12 months each, and Z invests for 7 months. If Z's share of the profit is Rs30,000, what is the total profit earned by the firm?

Ravi and Sita start a bakery business. Ravi invests $15,000 for 9 months. Sita invests $10,000 but joins after 3 months, investing for 6 months. At the end of the year, their business earns a total profit of $18,000. How much profit does Ravi receive?

Three friends Emily, Jake, and Lila decide to start a community gardening project. Emily contributes $10,000 and stays invested for the full 12 months. Jake contributes $8,000 but withdraws his investment after 6 months. Lila joins the project right after Jake exits, investing $12,000 for the remaining 6 months. At the end of the year, the project makes a total profit of $24,000. How should the profit be fairly distributed among Emily, Jake, and Lila based on their investment and duration?

A software testing agency is started by Daren and Elly. Daren invests $12,000 for 12 months, and Elly joins later, investing $9,000. At the end of the year, profits are shared equally. For how many months did Elly stay in the business?
M and N started a business with capital of Rs. 28,000 and Rs. 32,000. After 4 months, M withdrew Rs. 8,000 and N added Rs. 8,000. If at the end of the year, the profit was Rs. 54,000, find N's share.

P and Q started a business by investing a total of Rs. 9,600. If P invested for m months less than Q, then find the value of m given that P's profit share is Rs. 2,400. Also, P invested Rs. 1,600 less than Q, and the total profit earned at the end of the year is Rs. 9,600.

Three partners A, B, and C started a business. A invested Rs. 30,000, B invested Rs. 20,000, and C invested Rs. 25,000. After 6 months, A withdrew Rs. 5,000 and C invested Rs. 7,000 more. At the end of the year, the firm incurred a loss of Rs. 24,000. Find the share of loss borne by partner C.
X, Y, and Z invest Rs. 12,000, Rs. 15,000, and Rs. 18,000, respectively. X invests for 12 months, Y for 8 months, and Z for 10 months. If the total profit is Rs. 22,000, calculate Y's share.

Kamal and Harshit started a business by investing Rs. 4800 and Rs. 6000. After 4 months, Kamal added Rs. 1200 more and Harshit withdrew Rs. 1200 from their respective initial investment. If the annual profit earned by Harshit was Rs. 2600, then find the profit share of Kamal.
A and B start a business with investments of Rs. 18,000 and Rs. 24,000, respectively. If the profit-sharing ratio is 3 : 4, determine the ratio of the time periods for which A and B invested.

Three people named 'W', 'X' and 'Y' together start a business by investing Rs. 4a, Rs. 20,000, and Rs. 5b, respectively. 'W' invested his amount for 10 months, 'X' and 'Y' invested their amount for 2 more months than the 'W'. One year after starting the business, the sum of profit shares of 'X' and 'Y' was 35% more than that of 'W' and the share of 'X' was 80% of that of 'Y'. Find the sum of the initial investment of 'W' and 'Y'.
A and B start a business with capitals of Rs. 20,000 and Rs. 45,000. If the profit is divided in the ratio 2 : 3, determine the ratio of the time periods of investment for A and B.

'P' and 'Q' started a business by investing Rs. 20,800 and Rs. 16,000, respectively. After 'T' months later, 'R' joined them by investing 25% more than 'P'. If at the end of 1.5 years, the ratio of profit shares of 'Q', and 'R' is 12:13, respectively, then find the value of 'T'.

A and B start a business with capital of Rs. 25,000 and Rs. 35,000. After 5 months, C joins with Rs. 40,000. After another 3 months, A withdraws Rs. 5,000, and B adds Rs. 10,000, while C continues with the same. At the end of a year, the profit is Rs. 60,000. Find the exact share of profit of C, (within 2 decimal).
X and Y make investments of Rs40,000 and Rs60,000, respectively. After one year, the total profit, which includes a 10% interest, amounts to Rs16,000. What portion of this profit does X receive?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 02 Aug 2025, Shift 3

question: T and U start a business. T invests Rs40,000. After 3 months, U joins with Rs60,000. After 3 more months, T doubles his capital. If the business runs for 12 months, find the profit ratio of T to U.
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 24 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: M and N invest Rs80,000 and Rs1,20,000. After a year, the total profit, including 10% interest, is Rs33,000. What is N's share?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 25 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: P and Q invest Rs1,20,000 and Rs90,000 respectively in a business. After 4 months, P increases his capital by 50%. If the total profit after 1 year is Rs99,000, what is Q's share?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 28 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: P and Q invest Rs1,00,000 and Rs50,000, respectively. After one year, the total profit, which includes a 10% interest, amounts to Rs20,000. Determine Q's share of the profit.
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 29 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: X and Y start a business. X invests Rs75,000, Y invests Rs1,00,000. After 6 months, Y withdraws Rs40,000. If profit at year-end is Rs1,05,000, what is X's share?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 30 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: A, B, and C invest Rs40,000, Rs60,000, and Rs80,000, respectively. A invests for 12 months, B for 10 months, and C for 6 months. The total profit is Rs60,000. What is A's share of the profit?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 31 Jul 2025, Shift 3

question: A, B, C share a profit of Rs4800 in the ratio 3:4:5. If C's share is reduced by Rs400, and the remainder is equally divided between A and B, what will be A's final share ? ⚠️ links will not give you any mock test. Please use only the original PDF.
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 26 Jul 2025, Shift 2
